title: Convex
description: Open-source reactive backend-as-a-service with a TypeScript database, server functions, file storage, and real-time sync.
installation: This template deploys ONLY the Convex backend (ghcr.io/get-convex/convex-backend). It exposes two HTTP ports: 3210 is the client API / sync endpoint (containerPort, what you publish as CONVEX_CLOUD_ORIGIN) and 3211 serves HTTP actions / the 'site' origin (CONVEX_SITE_ORIGIN). Because Kubero routes a single ingress to one port, the public URL maps to 3210; if you need HTTP actions reachable externally, expose port 3211 with a second route or host and update CONVEX_SITE_ORIGIN accordingly. COMPANION SERVICE: deploy the Convex dashboard separately as its own app from image ghcr.io/get-convex/convex-dashboard:latest (listens on 6791), and point its NEXT_PUBLIC_DEPLOYMENT_URL at this backend's CONVEX_CLOUD_ORIGIN. Database: the bundled Postgres add-on holds all application data; note POSTGRES_URL must be the SERVER connection string WITHOUT a database path (Convex creates and manages its own database named after INSTANCE_NAME). DO_NOT_REQUIRE_SSL=true is set because the in-cluster Postgres uses plaintext; remove it if you front Postgres with TLS. ROTATE before going live: INSTANCE_SECRET (openssl rand -hex 32) and the Postgres password. FIRST RUN: after the backend is up, generate a deploy admin key with `kubectl exec <backend-pod> -- ./generate_admin_key.sh` (or `docker compose exec backend ./generate_admin_key.sh` style) and use it as CONVEX_SELF_HOSTED_ADMIN_KEY when running `npx convex deploy/dev` against CONVEX_SELF_HOSTED_URL (the 3210 origin). File storage defaults to the local persistent volume at /convex/data; for production-scale file/snapshot storage Convex can use S3-compatible object storage via the AWS_* / S3_STORAGE_*_BUCKET env vars (not configured here).
